title: TELC B2 Mock Test 1
description: Complete TELC B2 test with all 4 skills
instructions: Read the instructions for each part of the paper carefully. Answer all the questions. Write your answers in the answer sheet. You must complete the answer sheet within the time limit. At the end of the test, hand in both this question paper and your answer sheet.

words.definition: to travel regularly to and from work
words.example: Many people commute to the city for work.

words.definition: too much traffic in a place
words.example: Traffic congestion is a major problem in cities.

words.definition: designed for people to live in
words.example: The residential area is very quiet.

words.definition: something from the past that is important
words.example: The old building is part of our cultural heritage.

words.definition: to repair and improve a building
words.example: They plan to renovate the old house.

words.definition: able to continue over time without damage
words.example: We need sustainable solutions to environmental problems.

words.definition: something that encourages action
words.example: Tax breaks are an incentive for businesses.

words.definition: a chemical element found in fuels
words.example: Carbon emissions contribute to climate change.

words.definition: the release of gases into the air
words.example: The factory reduced its emissions.

words.definition: able to be replaced naturally
words.example: Solar energy is a renewable resource.

words.definition: a person who pays for a service
words.example: The newspaper has thousands of subscribers.

words.definition: the title of a news article
words.example: The headline caught my attention.

words.definition: the number of copies sold
words.example: The magazine's circulation has increased.

words.example: She works as a journalist for a local paper.

words.example: The editorial criticized the new policy.

words.definition: continuing for a long time
words.example: He suffers from chronic back pain.

words.definition: a sign of illness
words.example: Common symptoms include fever and cough.

words.definition: identifying an illness
words.example: The doctor made a quick diagnosis.

words.definition: to recommend medicine
words.example: The doctor will prescribe antibiotics.

words.definition: treatment for an illness
words.example: Physical therapy helps with recovery.

content.text: The new cycle lanes on Smith Street have caused controversy among local residents and commuters alike. While cyclists celebrate the new safety measures, some drivers complain that the reduced road space has made traffic worse during rush hour. The city council argues that the long-term benefits to public health and the environment outweigh the short-term inconvenience. They point to studies showing a 15% increase in cycling since the lanes were introduced.

Westfield Academy is now offering evening classes for adults who want to learn new skills. Subjects range from computer programming to ceramic art. Classes run from 6pm to 9pm on weekdays, with some weekend options available. The fees are reasonable, with discounts for seniors and the unemployed. Enrollment is open throughout the year, with new courses starting every month.

The historic Riverside Theatre, which has been a landmark since 1923, faces an uncertain future after the building was damaged in last month's storms. The theatre's owner has launched an urgent appeal for funds to carry out essential repairs. Without immediate action, the venue could close permanently. Local heritage groups have expressed concern, noting that the theatre is an important part of the town's cultural identity.

A new report from the Environment Agency suggests that recycling rates in the UK have reached an all-time high. The study attributes this success to better public education and improved collection services. However, the agency warns that more needs to be done to meet future targets. They recommend that manufacturers reduce packaging and that consumers think more carefully about what they buy.

GreenTech Solutions is seeking entrepreneurs to join their sustainable business incubator programme. The company offers workspace, mentorship, and access to funding for businesses focused on environmental solutions. Previous participants have developed products ranging from biodegradable packaging to solar-powered home systems. Applications are open until the end of the month.

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct heading for each paragraph.

questions.text: Paragraph 1
Paragraph 2
Paragraph 3
Paragraph 4
Paragraph 5

sections.title: Part 2: Detailed Understanding
sections.description: Read the article about remote work and answer the multiple-choice questions.

content.text: The concept of remote work has transformed dramatically over the past decade. What began as a privilege for a few has become a necessity for millions. Companies that once resisted flexible working arrangements have now embraced them, largely due to technological advances and changing employee expectations. The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated this trend, forcing businesses to adopt remote work policies virtually overnight.

One of the main benefits of remote work is the elimination of daily commutes. Employees save both time and money by working from home. Studies show that the average worker spends over an hour commuting each day. This time can be redirected towards work, family, or personal interests. Additionally, reduced commuting decreases carbon emissions, contributing to environmental sustainability.

However, remote work is not without its challenges. Many employees report feelings of isolation and difficulty maintaining work-life boundaries. The home environment can be full of distractions, from family members to household chores. Some workers also struggle with the lack of spontaneous interactions that occur in office settings, which can hinder collaboration and creativity.

Employers have also had to adapt their management styles. Traditional supervision methods, which often rely on physical presence, need to be reimagined. Many companies are now focusing on outcomes rather than hours worked. This shift requires greater trust between managers and their teams, as well as new performance metrics that measure productivity rather than time spent at a desk.

Looking to the future, experts predict that hybrid work models will become the norm. This approach combines the flexibility of remote work with the benefits of face-to-face collaboration. Companies are encouraged to develop clear policies that outline expectations for both remote and in-office days. The key is finding a balance that works for both the business and its employees.

questions.text: According to the article, what accelerated the adoption of remote work?
The article mentions that remote work helps the environment because
options: buildings are empty during the day

questions.text: One challenge of working from home mentioned in the article is
According to the article, employers need to
options: focus on outcomes rather than hours worked
questions.answer: focus on outcomes rather than hours worked

questions.text: The article suggests that the future of work will be

content.text: City General Hospital has announced plans to expand its emergency department due to increasing patient numbers. The expansion will include new treatment rooms and state-of-the-art equipment. Construction is expected to begin next spring and will take approximately eighteen months to complete. The hospital has also hired additional staff to reduce waiting times.

Sleep Smart - A new app that claims to improve your sleep quality by analyzing your patterns and offering personalized recommendations. The app tracks your sleep cycles using your phone's sensors and provides a detailed report each morning. Premium features include guided meditation and white noise sounds. Available now on iOS and Android.

The Community Health Trust is offering free health screenings at various locations throughout the month. Tests available include blood pressure, cholesterol, and diabetes checks. No appointment is necessary - simply drop in between 9am and 5pm. Results are confidential and will be discussed with you on the spot.

Mindful Moments - A new wellness centre opening next month will offer yoga, meditation, and alternative therapies. Classes are suitable for all ages and fitness levels. The centre aims to promote mental wellbeing alongside physical health. Special introductory rates are available for the first fifty members who sign up.

Nutritionist's Corner - Eating a balanced diet is essential for good health. This weekly column will explore different food groups and their benefits. This week: understanding protein sources and how much you really need. Expert advice from qualified nutritionists to help you make informed choices about what you eat.

The annual Charity Fun Run will take place in Richmond Park on the 15th of June. Participants can choose from 5k, 10k, or half-marathon distances. All proceeds will go to local children's hospitals. Registration is now open online, with early bird discounts available until the end of April.

Dr. Sarah Mitchell, a local GP, will be giving a free presentation on managing stress in the modern workplace. The event will be held at the Community Centre on May 20th at 7pm. Topics covered will include recognizing symptoms of stress and practical techniques for maintaining mental wellbeing. All are welcome to attend.

Fitness First gym has unveiled plans for a new rooftop workout area with stunning city views. The facility will include outdoor exercise equipment and a yoga studio. Members will be able to enjoy workouts in the fresh air while taking in the panorama. The new area is expected to open by summer.

subTasks.instruction: Which paragraph (A-H) contains the following information?

questions.text: You want to join a gym that offers outdoor exercise with views.
You want to visit a hospital that has expanded its emergency department.
You are looking for a free presentation about stress management.
You want to improve your sleep with a smartphone app.
You want to participate in a running event for charity.
You are interested in learning about nutrition.
You want a free health check without booking an appointment.
You are interested in yoga and meditation classes.

content.text:
Hi Sarah,

I hope you're doing well. I wanted to drop you a quick note to ask ____(21)____ your plans for the summer holidays. I remember you saying you wanted to travel somewhere, but I can't remember where.

I ____(22)____ been thinking about visiting my cousin in Barcelona. She ____(23)____ there for two years now and keeps telling me how amazing it is. The weather is supposed to be brilliant and there seems ____(24)____ be so much to see and do.

I was wondering whether you would like to come with me. We could share the cost of the accommodation and it would be much more fun ____(25)____ a friend to travel with. I know you ____(26)____ been to Spain before, so it would be nice to show you around.

Let me know what you think. If you're interested, I'll look ____(27)____ flights and hotels.

Best,
Emma

P.S. I ____(28)____ to book the accommodation yet, so there's no pressure!

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct answer for each gap.

She ____(23)____ there for two years now and keeps telling me how amazing it is.
The weather is supposed to be brilliant and there seems ____(24)____ be so much to see and do.
We could share the cost of the accommodation and it would be much more fun ____(25)____ a friend to travel with.
I know you ____(26)____ been to Spain before, so it would be nice to show you around.
If you're interested, I'll look ____(27)____ flights and hotels.
P.S. I ____(28)____ to book the accommodation yet, so there's no pressure!

sections.title: Language Elements, Part 2
sections.description: Read the following text and complete the gaps.

content.text:
Coffee is one of the world's most popular drinks, with millions of people ____(a)____ it every day. The story of coffee begins in Ethiopia, where it is said that a goat herder noticed his animals became very ____(b)____ after eating berries from a certain tree. From there, coffee spread to Arabia, where it ____(c)____ cultivated for the first time. By the 16th century, it had reached Persia, Egypt and Turkey.

Coffee ____(d)____ in Europe in the 17th century, brought by Venetian traders. Initially, the Catholic Church considered banning the drink, but Pope Clement VIII actually ____(e)____ the drink allowing it to be drunk by Catholics. The first coffee house opened in Venice in 1645.

Today, Brazil is the world's largest coffee ____(f)____, producing about a third of the global supply. Vietnam and Colombia are also major producers. The coffee industry supports millions of farmers worldwide, though many face ____(g)____ challenges such as climate change and ____(h)____ prices.

The most commonly grown species are Arabica and Robusta. Arabica is considered to have a smoother, more ____(i)____ taste, while Robusta is ____(j)____ and contains more caffeine. Regardless of preference, coffee remains an integral part of daily life for billions of people around the world.

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct word (a-o) to complete each sentence.

listening.title: Listening
listening.instructions: You will hear a number of different recordings and you will have to answer questions on what you hear. There will be time for you to read the instructions and questions, and you will have a chance to check your work. All the recordings will be played once only. The test is in 3 parts. At the end of the test, you will be given 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet.

sections.title: Situations
sections.subtitle: Part 1: Listening
sections.description:
You will hear people talking in eight different situations.
For questions 1-8, choose the best answer A, B or C.
sections.transcript:
1. Woman: So you’ve won the teenage chef competition, Mark – congratulations!
Mark: Thanks! In the competition, you choose whether to cook a meat, fish or vegetarian dish; then you’re judged on the taste and how attractive it looks. I couldn’t decide between fish and vegetables – I’ve never been brilliant at meat dishes. Then I found the judge was an expert in cooking fish, which worried me, so I went for my other choice – and won!

2. Girl: Excuse me. I think I left my book on this table – have you seen it?
Man: Oh yes. I took it inside with the empty glass when I cleared the table. I gave it to the manager, who put it with the other lost property. He keeps it all in the drawer of his desk.
Girl: 
Man: He’s on the phone inside the café. Why don’t you go inside – he’ll be finished in a minute.

3. Girl: Does your house seem empty now your sister’s left home, Josh?
Josh: Not really. My grandmother’s moved into her room, and she doesn’t go out much. It’s really nice having her there because my dad never comes home until later, and if my mum’s out for the evening, Grandma cooks supper for me.

4. Boy: Have you packed for your holiday?
Girl: No, I need to go shopping before I can do that. Last holiday, my suitcase handle got broken, so I need something new to pull all my stuff in. But suitcases are so heavy to carry.
Boy: I always take a backpack – you can carry more that way.
Girl: Yes, I thought about one of those, but good-quality ones are expensive. I’ll get one of those big sports bags. You know, one you can pull along. They don’t cost much, so it doesn’t matter if it gets torn.

5. Man: The train arriving at platform six in the 11.45 from Manchester. The train due to arrive at platform four in approximately five minutes is the delayed 11.30 train to Manchester. The train will now depart at 11.50 and travel non-stop. We apologise to passengers for the delay. Refreshments will not be available on this train.

6. Kimberley: Hi, it’s Kimberley. I got your message and I’d love to go to the movies. There’s a film called SpyGame, or a comedy – I don’t mind which … Shall we meet outside the cinema?
Girl: Or, I know, at the burger bar on the corner. We could have a snack before we go in or we could meet at the bus station.
Kimberley: Mum’s driving me into town this afternoon, to buy some trainers, so I won’t be near the station. But something to eat first is a good idea. Shall we say 6 o’clock?
Girl: OK.

7. Boy: Have you been to the new water sports centre yet?
Girl: Oh yes, it’s brilliant. There are two indoor pools, one for underwater diving and one for swimming, and you can also have sailing lessons on the lake.
Boy: That’s what I’m doing there next weekend, actually. I was hoping to take waterskiing lessons, but the leaflet says they’re not starting those until next year.

sections.title: Interview: Nick Parker of Krispy
sections.subtitle: Part 2: Listening
sections.description:
You will hear an interview with a singer called Nick Parker who plays in a band called Krispy with his sister Mel.
First you will have one minute to read the questions.
Then you will hear the interview.
Decide if each statement is TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
sections.transcript:
Int: I’m talking to Nick Parker, the singer with the band called Krispy. Nick your sister, Mel, plays guitar in the band too, doesn’t she?

Nick: Yeah. Mel’s a year younger than me. We’ve been playing and singing together since we were … eight, nine. Dad is a guitarist and took us to hear the great bands playing live. Mel and I put on shows at school, which was a lot of fun. Mum thought we were good, but she didn’t want us to get too serious about our music because of the hard lives professional musicians have.

Int: When did you start writing music?

Nick: I’ve been writing since I was ten. And later Mel started working with me. We didn’t have the same influences – I liked rock music and she loved world music, especially bands from Africa. But we found good ways of mixing the styles.

Int: Your band, Krispy, has two guys and two girls in it. How was it formed?

Nick: Mel and I were playing in a concert at our college and there were two students from music school in the audience. They came to see us after the show and asked if we’d like to form a band with them. We weren’t sure at first because we were much younger than them. But we agreed to try it out, and it was brilliant.

Int: Was the band an immediate success?

Nick: Well, we spent the first year practising and writing music. During that time we all had studying to do. We played in local concerts, and the audiences enjoyed what we did. Then during one holiday we recorded two songs and sent them to a music company. They offered us a contract, but our parents said we had to finish college first.

Int: You’ve been together a few years now and you’re one of the top bands. What’s that like?

Nick: Hard work. We travel to concerts all round the world and are never in one place for more than a few nights. The others are like an older brother and sister to me and Mel, which is good. They help us relax on our days off, and make sure we eat well. They’re strict about practising too.

Int: Have you had any disappointments?

Nick: Everything we’ve recorded has done well. Three singles have gone to number one, and our first album has sold over a million copies. Our second album was due out this winter, but I’ve been ill recently so we’ve started recording late, which is a pity. But for the rest, everything’s fine.
sections.context: Interview with Nick Parker of the band Krispy

subTasks.instruction: Decide if each statement is TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

questions.text: Nick’s father is a musician.
Nick’s mother encouraged him to become a professional musician.
Nick and Mel have always liked the same kind of music.
Nick and Mel were invited to form a band by two older students.
The band Krispy got a contract with a music company after their first year together.
The other two members of the band help Nick and Mel a lot.
The band has had problems with their record sales.

sections.title: Course on Wildlife
sections.subtitle: Part 3: Listening
sections.description:
You will hear a man called Ben, from a young people’s organisation, telling a youth group about a course they can do on Saturdays.
For questions 15-20, choose the correct answer A, B or C.
sections.transcript:
Ben: Hello, everyone. Let me introduce myself – my name’s Ben and I’d like to tell you about a course you can do on Saturday mornings.

I’m from an organisation called ‘Nature’ and we run courses for young people like you to do during the weekends. Now, we know that at school you learn a lot about things like science and maths, but this course is really about giving your youth group the chance to come and find out about wildlife. It’s an area that tends to get forgotten.

We usually take groups like yours to a wild place that’s near their home. Sometimes we go to a beach or a lake, but we’ve planned for your group to go to a forest. That’s the closest place for you, and the easiest to get to.

Now, we’d like you to come and do some activities with us. You spend six hours each week with us and the course lasts for 12 weeks, depending on the weather. That might sound like a long time, but we think you’ll be sorry when it’s over!

Taking part in our activities means you get to do all sorts of things that you wouldn’t normally be allowed to do at home. That includes how to safely climb trees, cut wood and build a fire.

While you’re with us, we’ll also show you how to make things out of different materials. On the last course, everyone designed backpacks which they were very pleased with. This time we thought we’d get you to design and make a birdhouse. When it’s finished, you can take it home and show your family.

We think you’ll enjoy doing this.

Now, any questions …?
sections.context: Ben from a young people's organization talks about a Saturday course

subTasks.instruction: For questions 15-20, choose the correct answer A, B or C.

sections.title: Introduction and Interview
sections.subtitle: Part 1: Speaking
sections.instruction: You are expected to give a short presentation to your partner(s) on one of the following topics. (The prompts in brackets may help you.) Your presentation should take about 90 seconds and you should answer your partner's questions afterwards. While your partner is giving his/her presentation, listen and think of the questions you would like to ask. You should not interrupt your partner during the presentation

topics.topic: A holiday destination you would recommend

questions.text: Why would you recommend this place?

topics.topic: A job you would like to do in the future

questions.text: What qualifications would you need?

sections.title: Discussion
sections.subtitle: Part 2: Speaking
sections.instruction: Read the following text from a magazine. Discuss the content of the text with your partner(s). Tell them your opinions; give reasons and personal examples to support your ideas. Talk about your own experience with the problems mentioned and possible solutions.
sections.text: The use of smartphones among young people has become a growing concern for parents and educators alike. Studies show that teenagers spend an average of four hours per day on their devices, with many reporting feelings of anxiety when separated from their phones. While technology offers undeniable benefits, excessive use has been linked to sleep problems, reduced face-to-face social interaction, and decreased academic performance. Some schools have implemented phone-free zones, but is this enough?

questions.text: What are the benefits and drawbacks of smartphone use for young people?
Should schools restrict phone usage? Why or why not?
How can parents help their children develop healthy technology habits?
What would you suggest to reduce problematic phone use?

sections.title: Collaborative Task
sections.subtitle: Part 3: Speaking
sections.instruction:
You and your partner have been asked to advise the local council on how to make the town centre more environmentally friendly.

Firstly, think of some ideas to include in your presentation.

Then, discuss your ideas with your partner(s) and try to agree on the three most important suggestions.

questions.text: Which suggestions would have the biggest impact?
What are the costs and benefits of each suggestion?
Which three ideas should be prioritised?

sections.title: Task 1: Letter
sections.subtitle: Part 1: Writing
sections.instruction:
Write a formal letter of complaint. In your letter write in detail about
a) at least three of the following points
or
b) at least two of the following points plus one other aspect related to the topic:
• What you bought and when
• What the problem was
• How you tried to resolve the issue
• What resolution you expect

Before you start writing, decide on the order of the points. Use a suitable greeting and complimentary closing. Start your letter with a few opening sentences and end the letter politely with one or two closing sentences.

Please write at least 150 words.
sections.context:
Customer Services Manager
ElectroPlus Ltd
123 High Street
Manchester M1 5AB

Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to complain about a laptop I purchased from your store on 15th March. Within two weeks of purchase, the laptop began showing serious problems including frequent freezing, battery not charging properly, and unusual noises from the fan. I have tried restarting the device multiple times and have also attempted to run the troubleshooting software, but the issues persist.

I returned to the store and spoke to a member of staff who told me the warranty had expired and that I would need to pay for any repairs. I believe this is unacceptable as the laptop was sold to me as a new product and should be working properly.

I would like a full refund or a replacement laptop.
sections.prompt: Write a formal letter of complaint about a faulty product.
bullets: How you tried to resolve the issue

sections.title: Task 2: Letter
sections.subtitle: Part 2: Writing
sections.context: Your friend has just moved to a new city and feels lonely and homesick. Write a letter to your friend to give them advice on how to make friends in their new environment and how to deal with feeling homesick.
sections.prompt: Write a letter to a lonely friend giving advice on making friends and dealing with homesickness.
bullets: Give advice on making new friends
Offer tips on dealing with homesickness
